sections.paragraph:
Original: נכל

Transliteration: nâkal

Phonetic: naw-kal'

BDB Definition:

  1. to be deceitful, be crafty, be knavish
    1. (Qal)
      1. to be knavish
      2. deceiver (participle)
    2. (Piel) to beguile
    3. (Hithpael) to act craftily, act knavishly, deal knavishly

Origin: a primary root

TWOT entry: 1366

Part(s) of speech: Verb
